channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Today someone said he makes $125k a year and then another person said >what do you do to make $125k >that is a lot >are… — 199 replies — 796.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today someone said he makes $125k a year and then another person said >what do you do to make $125k >that is a lot >are…

Open thread • Replies: 199 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:52 UTC

OP

Today someone said he makes $125k a year and then another person said
>what do you do to make $125k
>that is a lot
>are you in LA?
>why are you lying?
Then I said
>150k is middle class
>no offense, when others don’t believe others, it means they are poor or make less
Then he replied
>150k is not middle class
>60k-80k is middle class
I said
>$125k cannot afford a middle class lifestyle and most jobs paying $125k are in big cities and there is high cost of living
Then he starts lying and saying he is 13
The conversation is just him seething someone makes $125k and arguing that 150k is not middle class.
I tell him due to increased cost of living and wealth gap, even 1m is nothing and 10% of 50% of the consumption and 90% are not doing well.
Why are poor people always seething?
$125k isn’t even middle class. How poor do you guys that guy is? 40k? 70k?
He was really defensive about $150k being middle class.
Do poor people think they are middle class?
This isn’t the first time this has happened.

Early replies (first 3)

61965995
>>61965964
Middle class is self sufficient, it requires an income that can support a household, retirement, children, education(including college), stay at home wife (or) nanny, 2+ modern cars, insurance, 1-3 vacations/yr . I would say 125k-250k depending on area. Lower class normies think they are middle class, middle class is mostly dead. Upper class has excess income after meeting those needs. Lower class requires gov assistance(welfare, healthcare, public school, etc).

61966005
>>61965964
>>$125k cannot afford a middle class lifestyle and most jobs paying $125k are in big cities and there is high cost of living
When people ssay middle class they mean they've got a wife and together they make about 140k or something and can easily afford a suburban house in areas within 2 hours of major cities, especially in southern and midwestern cities like Omaha and Dallas. Your idea of middle class is jetsetting around and banging sloots whenever you want.

61966016
>>61965995
> 1-3 vacations/yr .
1 vacation, maybe dude.
3? Lmao.
I think middle class lifestyle now requires a lot higher income than what people think now.
That wasn’t the first time someone got really defensive with “no, 60-80k is middle class.”
Fucking annoying but good conversation I guess. The ignorance is annoying.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61968302
Class is not derived from monthly income level. If you think it is, that's a giveaway that you're working class.

Middle class people own their home outright, no debts, and don't have to worry about work. IE if they lose their job, they could support themselves for at least a full year without incurring a hit to their standard of living, or getting into turmoil.

Middle class people can work fun jobs for low income, and still live middle class lives, because they have their home paid off. Higher middle class will own stocks that provide a stable income for themselves as well as their home.

If you have to work for a living, you are working class. Regardless of what your takehome is.

If not going to work means you lose your home, you're definitely not middle class.

61968307
>too stupid to understand cost of living
Sorry for the people conversing with you

61969189
>>61967708
They're doing that a lot lately
Apparently 4chins is personalstreetshitterblog central
The really sad thing is the post volume this bait generates. Look at these post numbers!
sage for off topic shit thread

61969890
>>61968302
MIDDLE CLASS, the bottom 70%, has no savings.
>>61969189
The topic is good for debating. Everyone has their own definition of middle class. Fuck off.

61969976
>>61968302
>Middle class people own their home outright, no debts, and don't have to worry about work.
seems like a pretty naive view of credit

in my experience wealthy people use credit as much or more than the less rich. Going into debt is probably necessary to becoming wealthy, and even after you're rich it's still better to risk the bank's money than your own for most stuff.

also again, Buy Borrow Die implies using debt to avoid taxes. And it's nearly universal

It is fun to read how angery 4chan users get about debt though. I've never met a wealthy person that won't borrow money. But 4chan thinks having debt is somehow bad, or a sign of failure.

61970001
>>61969976
You’re an old pos living in some ruralfag area.

61970023
>>61969890
>overly taxonomizing petty subclasses is good debate
Wew lad
>>61970001
>good debate

No matter how hard life gets for me, at least I'll never be this clueless, classless, or crude. God damn it feels good having a soul. I'm taking all of your izzat and going home. Come at me ((You) can't, and (You) shan't)

61970145
>>61966005
>2 hours of major cities, especially in southern and midwestern cities like Omaha
You can live under 30 min from Omaha for cheap in 100% white towns.
>t. bought a house 30 min from 72nd street and I-80 on $24/hour in 2018

61970160
>>61970145
> You can live under 30 min from Omaha for cheap in 100% white towns.
aka lower class areas.

61970170
>>61970145
The entire discussion was about how people that struggle in the big city should move out the city. Then here you are moving out the city because you cannot afford it. Do people have 0 self awareness?

61970185
>>61967097
> 95/100 times someone who owns a million dollar house is upper class.
Not in big cities or any city that is well known. 1m would be considered ghetto and low class.

61970313
>>61970185
To add to this
A large fraction of homeowners are also self employed business owners

It's really easy to own a half million dollar house and a half million dollar business and be a working class millionaire

61970322
>>61970313
I think most millionaires are working class. 1/10 have 1m. Of that 10%, 2.5% have 1m excluding a home.
So 99% of Americans are working class.

61970369
>>61965964

i speed raced my income from about $80k - $170k in 5 years. never felt so rich. last 5 years i'm barely up $20k. definitely below inflation. but job is comfy lazy wfh - so i stick with it. am i wrong?

61970371
>>61970369
What? up 20k?

61970415
>>61966076
My household income is around 100k and i live in a relatively low cost of living area and i cant afford a house 3 bedroom 2 bathroom 1500+ sqft house. If you cant afford a house you're not middle class and 25k extra sure as shit isnt going to jump you up 2 spots

61970454
>>61970415
So you are saying $60-80k is not middle class.
How come this topic is so polarizing and sparks this much debate? (read this thread)
$150k is the new middle class.

61970484
>>61967122
Just lie on your application i make less than 70k and have 9 credit cards the highest limit is nearly 20k. The issuers have no way to confirm your income so i just put 130k and raise it a bit every year

61970502
>>61970454
To be middle class you need to marry and let your wife have a career

Neither idea is popular here

61970516
>>61970502
Marriage is cucked in 2026.
If you want kids, find a way without marrying.
If you want female companionship, get a hooker for a few hours.

61970523
>>61970502
A girl in a career = girl who went to college and spends 40 hours a week at work. Gross.
Stay at home wife = bitch living for free. No.

61970557
>>61970454
Thats the thing i dont consider myself middle class because i cant afford a house, but if i would just put another 10k over asking on one of the house i put an offer on 5 years ago when house values were still reasonable and interest rates were half current rate i would consider myself middle class. Middle class varies by too many factors to tie to a single number. I would also consider myself middle class if i was a full coiner

61970564
>>61970516
>>61970523
Exactly

Middle class is out of reach for almost everyone your age because you're either unwilling or incapable of doing what needs done to get there.

61970580
>>61970557
> Middle class varies by too many factors to tie to a single number.
LMAO. THIS is why this topic gets so many replies. Middle class implies a lifestyle. Then you find out how much it costs. Simple.

61970586
>>61970564
Shut up.

61970595
>>61970586
Stupid bot programmed to respond to every comment

61970642
>>61965964
The middle class are just rich people with poor financial sense.
They finance like three vehicles, constantly buy new yard toys(mowers, blowers, fourwheelers, etc.) and stay in eternal debt slavery to up the debt slave score.

61970911
>>61967708
at least its 4c content and not reposts from twitter

61971594
>>61965964
Most Americans want to lump themselves in middle class when in reality they are working class people in denial. Marx might've had a lot of retarded ideas but his definitions of class make a lot more sense then vaguely defining lifestyles and their relation to income.

Lumpenproles are nigs, broke NEETS, druggies and other welfare queens, proles are the majority of people who absolutely must labor in order to maintain their living standards long term, petit bourgeoisie have either enough capital that they can live on the profits generated or have a highly valuable skill set that one can largely dictate the course of their labor, but still must ultimately labor, and the bourgeoisie who have fuck you levels of money & power.

61972335
>>61970586
Lmao mad

item.title: Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT — 93 replies — 372.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:51 UTC

OP

You may all sperg the fuck out now. Never fucking selling.

Early replies (first 3)

61964214
>ZOG Swift intern casually poops LLM-generated report
>CHAINCHINK IS BOOMING BROS
actually delusional. $9.

61964222
don't care don't care
price price price
you lost the game, stinkies
PERIOD
stop trying to reclaim some shred of lost dignity. just take the heckin' L

61964252
>>61964222
i dont really give a fuck about link but this image disgusts me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61968298
>>61965461
we're pretty close. xStocks is the tech Kraken will be using to tokenize normal stocks from nasdaq, after nasdaq has entered partnership with them
Now guess what, 2025

>https://backed.fi/news-updates/xstocks-are-going-live-tokenized-stocks-for-the-defi-era

another one here:

OKX got a $25b buyout from NYSE mother company intercontinental exchange as strategic partnership.

Now guess what:

X Layer, the official eth L2 from OKX joined chainlink scale program in 2025. Basically, they agree to use chainlinks services for their stack for free usage of those services (CCIP, functions, automations, price feeds)

>https://web3.okx.com/learn/chainlink-xlayer

Not to mention chainlinks direct partnerships of publishing trade data of IC and NYSE.

Now look further. Competitors start using the same tools (Chainlink) because if you don't you get left behind, the biggest dex on solana adopts chainlink as their official oracle service for their upcoming prediction market, while polymarket has also adopted chainlink as official oracle service already months ago.

61968311
>>61968298
news like these will keep coming and before you know it, everything loops back to chainlink in some way.

I'm not saying ever service will use the FULL stack of services chainlink provides. Some might only use the price feeds.

others might only use functions or ccip

What matters is that chainlink put itself in a position, where most companies will adopt it out of principle. Imagine you want to spin up a company that wants to have the edge in one of those things (interoperability, speed, etc) but you have to sacrifice other things because you can't be the best in everything. So you fallback on using a proven company (chainlink) for the rest, it reduces complexity and you can focus on your own niche. Win win for everyone

61968320
>>61965559
This needs to be updated to swift runs endless pocs, never charges a fee and staking doesn't happen.
>$8

61968327
>>61968298
>>61968311
>Ctrl+f "LINK token functionality in all this technobabble
>0 results
can we finally ban linkjeets

61968340
So when will Chainlink become a publically traded company people can invest in?

61968358
>>61968340
CL employees have officially stated that there is no IPO happening, buy LINK token

61968601
>>61968340
they get equity in link token

61968616
>>61964682
how the fuck does swift even make money it feels like the biggest vaporwave company to ever exist..a big paper tiger.

61968817
>>61964199

saw some homeless guy ranting about this yesterday
he was crawling down the sidewalk trying to pickup food for his cult master, but wasn't making much progress, as he was using up all his energy yelling about these "partnerships"

i guess that goes to show you kids....cuckoldry does NOT pay


THE Cuckolds of crypto

61968828
>>61965559
>Swift going live.

wait i thought it went "live" in november of last year?

>>61965423
and worst of all, even in 10 years, none of that shit involves link funding token. it will be gone by then, mark my words

61968875
>>61964278
>look at all the XRP retards in the comments


here's the exact part on the chart where link boyscouts finally broke and went full psycho against xrp

this was the summer of last year ('25) when xrp ran up to mid $3's while link barely budged


you see, link, just like its holders, is so weak and limp, that even the entire market pumping can barely move it. it's like trying to drag a 400 pound dead body

it's funny to see a coin price mimic the physicality of its holders. art imitating life if you will

61968895
>>61968358
>CL employees have officially stated that there is no IPO happening

posts like these are great. they truly show how incredibly naive and inexperienced a link boyscout is

>duuuuuuude the janitor at the building i work at said the owner isn't selling

as if any of these conversations would involve a 26 yr old trans "god" who's job it is to copy and paste buzzwords on x

61968914
>>61968875
didnt read your slop lol Whatever you wrote still doesnt justify the XRP spammers being clinically retarded

61968946
>>61968616
>how the fuck does swift even make money

swift's purpose is not to make money. it's a co-op

they do collect some annual fees from the member banks to facilitate operations. they are not aiming to generate a profit, and they aren't taking a cut of cross border transactions if that's what you're thinking


not for nothing bro but you could've had answer to your q in like 15 seconds w ai search

61968951
>>61968914
>Whatever you wrote still doesnt justify the XRP spammers being clinically retarded

the funny part is you are the exact same as them

in your head your ego is telling you that you're a secret genius while the xrp cult just doesn't "get it". but the reality is they just went 7x last year while you made nothing

your ego is saving you from the brutal reality

61969258
>>61968951
so which one are you

61969267
>literal retard brown mentally ill gooners are making more money than you
feels bad man
but pain is temporary, glory is eternal! WAGMI MARINES

61969348
>>61964199
It's amazing the lack of interest in LINK. It's get the best news and doesn't move at all. In 2019 this news would have pumped it to $100.

61969656
>>61968951
>the funny part is you are the exact same as them

Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story. Token price doesnt matter here. Youre hung up on it only because it’s the only way you can downplay the fact that Chainlink got mentioned while XRP didnt lol. And just because link didn’t pump doesnt change the fact that Swift mentioned Chainlink.

61969683
>>61969656
>Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story
yup

61969746
>>61965107
Take your delusional ramblings back to /pol/ you fuckin freak

61969776
>>61967953
no, it's a guy with severe mental illness who has been posting the same image occasionally in link threads for many years. he's probably been extremely blown the fuck out on whatever else he put his money into and can only get satisfaction by making fun of stinkies, because he didn't buyed when it was below a dollar

61969981
>>61969656
>Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story.

and? bro you're cheering on an offshore company that you own zero equity in just like a sportsball fan

then you had to project me as some big xrp holder. i fucking wish. i actually bought the line that it was dead and didn't buy any this cycle

they used to say to always inverse biz, i should've listened

61969992
>>61969683
>>Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story
>yup

you meant to say chainlink labs i believe


this doesn't help the funding token tho kid. you guys keep trying these marketing strategies that haven't worked for 5 years straight.

i take it you've never read what einstein had to say on repeating the same thing over and over

61970155
>>61969981
>and?
I didnt read anything you wrote except the first word.

61970161
>>61964199
>but chainlink was MENTIONED in the swift files

61970974
>>61965559
I don't understand how this fucking chat works. Someone spoonfeed me

61971436
>>61964199
>Price feeds that do zero for token price
>ONLY 20 YEARS TIL PRELIMINARY PRODUCT ROLLOUT!!!! (no exaggeration exactly what SWIFT said)

61972300
>>61968875
>>61968914
>>61970155
Since the Trump pump.

61972330
Cainlink psychosis episode 5789.
Yeah, if there’s ever another huge crypto hype cycle, then ‘muh Chainlink actually getting mentioned somewhere in something serious’ might trigger a massive hype wave and your coin might go to the moon - and hopefully you’ll cash out fast enough.
But honestly, this is getting very cult-like rn.

item.title: WTF — 70 replies — 280.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF

Open thread • Replies: 70 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:47 UTC

OP

YOU SAID TO BUY OIL YOU SAID IRAN WAS GOING TO CLOSE THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ FUCK ALL OF YOU

Early replies (first 3)

61959820
The usa ran out of weapons lol. the strait is still fucked.

61959822
>>61959817
I didn't say that, you listened to someone else, should have listened to me retard

61959823
No refunds my brown friend

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61967187
>>61967054
Even if they stop the mines it doesn't matter because they can still missile strike the ships

61967237
>>61967187
>missile
You mean drone. It's a brand new world militarily, and 20k drone can down a tanker.

61967398
>>61966543
>btc longs
why lol. BTC such a simple winning hand - just buy and hodl. i guess people just gotta have a feeling they've outsmarted the consensus.

61967401
>>61967237
just get the us military to escort the tankers

61967443
>>61967401
They'll be sunk, there's a reason the carriers are out in the open ocean where they can maneuver.

61967479
>>61960453
Yeah, but then the choice for Trump is a forever war in Iran or leaving Israel high and dry and then having to hope the Iranians succeed with a decapitation strike on the entire Likud and Mossad at the same time or else whatever blackmail they have on him via Epstein is coming out. So uh I guess enjoy forever war in Iran and look forward to higher oil prices

61967481
>>61967401
>just get the most expensive fleet in the world to constantly go through a 39 km-96 km wide traphole to get droned and missiled to death
Nigga, do you want a modern amerifat version of the battle of the Bismarck Sea?

61967487
I don't get it. Why doesn't USA just tactical nuke the Iranian settlements near the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint?

61967497
Market has been down all fucking day. So much so that most of my stores didn't even want to put in any order or preload. Fucking up my day and pissing off my drivers who then piss me off.

>.t fuel dispatcher

61967498
>>61967487
Because that would release the tactinuke genie (so the ruskies would consider just nuking the ukrainians for example), make the US a pariah state (causing amongst other things a deep global economic depression) and cause a a retarded pursuit of nuclear power around the globe that would most likely result in some utterly disastrous nuclear conflict.

61967503
>>61967497
Tell the stores that /biz/ says oil going to $250

61967510
>>61967497
What, can't price gouge at $85 oil? lol

61967556
>>61967510
Pfff we wholesale ship this shit. It can be $1.50 or $18 we make the same.

61967565
>>61959833
>>61961565
>>61959887
>>61959956
IRGC isn't going to fight unless americans land and americans won't land because we have the fucking sea. IRGC benefits from basically usurping the clerics and taking over the country, with Khamenei Jr. being a puppet.

The guy hasn't even been seen since the 28th so he's probably dead.

61967677
Drain your pool, you have a delivery of crude comming!

61967697
>>61959817
Don't bet against America

61967744
>>61967697
i wont, but ill hope and pray that Persia is the most annoying, costly and drawn out war even if it makes me become poor

61967748
>>61967697
America bets against itself

61968398
>>61959956
By your logic Iran should have surrendered when Trump expected them to. Whoops.

61969202
>>61967677
checked, rekd and kekked

61969285
>>61959957
Reverse-capital strike to keep Trump's numbers up so he delivers them to the neofeudal promised land they backed him for

61971799
>>61967697
????????

61972015
>>61959820
Did Jesus run out of angels?

61972026
>>61968398
you sound copeful & deranged

61972239
>>61967039
They're not mining it. They don't need to plus they're letting their own ships go through to China

61972252
>>61967054
>blow up random fishing boats that weren't doing anything
>say mission accomplished
American moment

61972267
>>61972252
What’s wrong with blowing up random brownies pajeet?

61972312
>>61972267
So edgy

61972321
>>61972252
>dey dindu nuffin

61972353
>>61972321
You're the "dindu" on the world stage

item.title: WHO THE FUCK KEEPS PUMPING THIS SHIT — 52 replies — 208.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHO THE FUCK KEEPS PUMPING THIS SHIT

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:58 UTC

OP

WHO THE FUCK KEEPS PUMPING THIS SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

61963811
People with way more money than you

61963820
>>61963788
Me.

>>61963811
/thread

61963866
>>61963788
Michael Saylor will keep buying billions of dollars worth of Bitcoin every day while you're shorting.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61964079
>>61964042
It's all extremely clear if you have even a remedial understanding of TA.

61964089
>>61964007
other way around. crying first then rubbing hands

61964097
>>61964042
Bobo opens shorts, shorts cause market to drop, bobo closes shorts, causes market to rise, mumu buys the dip, bobo opens new shorts, rinse and repeat.

61964107
>>61964036
It doesn't affect the US (the only market that matters)

61964124
Demons

61964180
>>61963969
True. The market still thinks this is another 12 day war.

61964186
>>61964180
because it is you stupid shit, yesterday trump literally said it was nearly complete

61964188
>>61964026
>5 years no stop of dumps and crabdumps

We just had an ath in october.

61964212
>>61964186
That's what the orange nigger said, it's obvious everything went out of his control.

61964216
>>61963788
Why would I stop buying? Even if the war goes on for years the market will just keep going up with small dips here and there. I am here to make money long term. Not short term gambler highs.

61964226
>>61964212
>DURR THE WAR ISNT ENDING BECAUSE I SAID SO
actually fucking delusional, I guess the price of oil is still $120 in your mind. the market is already beyond recovering

61964243
Bitcoin was in a bear market prior to any issues with Iran. Even if it is cooling off (its not, iran is bombing at an even higher frequency) everything is even more bearish than it was before it all even started.

61964249
>ultra bad PR when they killed those schoolgirls meaning even less support for the war than usual
>high oil price fucks everyone so they'll just focus on that and liberate the strait
>kikes and their golem both say they already won
>orange nigger doesn't want dow under 50k
It's just up at this point until some new epstein distraction then AI bubble collapse.
Yes I panic sold the first day but luckily bought back cheaper.

61964259
>>61963788
the kikes that have been dumping have been killed by iranian missiles
the true bullrun can now resume

61964268
>>61964259
>the true bullrun can now resume
I'm so tired

61964312
>>61963788
its the agents currency now

61964492
>>61964188
We literally did not
Did you forget we printed like half of all the USD that has ever existed since 2021?
It was an ath in fake, gay, globohomo normie valuation

61965010
>>61964186
>yesterday trump literally said

I want you to picture for a moment the probability of him admitting that he fucked up and things aren't actually under control. If you actually believe he won't just go all "mission accomplished" the way Bush did after six weeks in Iraq, there's something wrong with you

61965024
>>61965010
why are the markets green and oil is cheap now then you stupid fucking idiot.

61965059
>>61965024
Because retail believed that shit, you fucking nigger.

61965066
>>61965059
>Major financial institutions are retail now
lmao, you dont see companies panicking or laying off anybody. nvidia just ejaculated a couple billion into another AI firm. everything is back to normal.

61965082
>>61965066
repeat that last sentence out loud

61965675
>>61964492
Even adjusted for inflation, bitcoin ath was on october 6

61965683
>>61965082

61965857
>>61963937
There is no oil shock

61965967
>>61964259
Damn.

61967725
>>61965857
Yeah, 20% of supply was cut off, this is totally normal and happens twice a month. Retard.

61967737
>>61967725
we're bypassing hormuz you stupid shit. there is no oil crisis

61968021
>>61967737

It was surely that easy the whole time bro

61972348
>>61967737
That pipeline is 7 million BPD. Hormuz is 20 million BPD.

item.title: POOL'S CLOSED — 49 replies — 67.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
POOL'S CLOSED

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:16 UTC

OP

POOL'S CLOSED

Early replies (first 3)

61967844
Doesn't matter, oil has been diverted.
Lol oil baggie

61967846
>oil dumps to $60 anyway
this is a nothingburger buy the shit and piss 500 dip and enjoy a 3x by next year

61967882
Inshallah someone will buy my bags now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61969332
>>61969265
>>61969303
You deserve to die too. I hope your death is as stupid as the ones that died in the OP.

61969346
>>61969317
>>61969332
nobody likes iran

nobody cares if they get attacked. They have zero friends that aren't suicidal religious fanatics

61969352
>>61969346
I'm not fond of Iran, but in your small burger brain if I'm not in the side of united states, i'm with Iran.
Just kill yourself at this point.

61969368
*yawn*
destroy everything already or fuck off

61969374
>>61969352
>everyone that disagrees with ME must die!
ok
that's pretty much iran's mistake.

they could sit back and look like the victims and people would feel bad for them

or they can attack everyone and guarantee nobody supports them. Just like you, they're not smart enough to sway anyone's opinion.

61969425
>>61969374
>or they can attack everyone and guarantee nobody supports them.
Except Israel does exactly that and they are one of the most successful countries. Look into the Samson Option.

61969431
>>61969425
>Samson Option
People still believe that’s a thing?

61969440
This tiny strip of land is the reason why ships have to go around Ash Shishah. Hilariously this would be a safer route if it were an island. A peninsula with no road because of steep mountains. Just a dead end hiking trail to to it. The only way to get there is by boat.

61969445
>>61969425
israel claims to be attacked with model rockets every day. I don't know if it's true or not, but the average reader probably believes it and doesn't mind them responding even when it's clearly disproportionate.

>weakling picks fights with the big guy and gets his ass kicked
>tries to play victim
>nobody falls for it

israel might be unpopular but islamic republics are way less popular

61969468
>>61969374
>feel bad about them
Wow, that sure helps.
Also, nobody actually cares that the Gulf monarchies are getting hit, they are disgusting. Except, of course, oil/gas wise.

61969481
>>61969445
Only americans and indians categorically hate muslims because they've eaten at the propaganda slop trough their whole lives.

61969487
>>61967838
>kill current leader trying to cause a regime change with a revolution and attack iran's oil refineries
>act surprised when people don't rise, the new leader is mad because you killed their family and they don't gain anything for stopping the war since you destroyed their refineries
Honestly, jews should have seen this one coming, but they needed their blood sacrifice for Purim i guess

61969511
>>61969481
only a muslim would fail to differentiate between the republic and the religion

>oh they unfairly hate muslims!
>we're the victims here!

no, we hate your government. It is evil. And you're evil for supporting it. Regardless of your religion.

61970002
>>61967838
Schronigger's pool

61970038
>>61969314
It’s not priced in, boomers and zionists are emotionally tied to Iran being a weak country and this being a nothing burger. It’s like how the Indians on this board are emotionally tied to their shitcoins making them billionaires.

61970064
>>61969481

It just doesn't sound appealing. White men like a healthy amount of freedom to do their own thing. We can see Islam is forced homogenization and a sort of dictatorial system with little wiggle room.

61970073
>>61969440
>just a dead end hiking trail leading to it
Oh damn... sigh... time to add another place to the (way too long) hiking vacation wishlist.

61970079
>>61967838

61970114
>>61967838

61970131
>not my problem
>不是我的问题

61970207
>>61970131
They have the mandate of heaven.

61970278
>>61967895
Why does wartime Israel look like peacetime America?

61970295
>>61970278
LET IT SPILL OVER INTO THE STREETS

61971446
>>61967844
They can't even begin to divert to match the deficit let alone the multi-day supply drag they are behind on. WAY too little WAY too late.

61971630
>>61969445
I know you are probably a yehudim so your POV is skewed, but more people sympathize with Iran than you think. Especially since they also bombed all the neo-liberal hellscapes in the gulf

61971644
>>61970114
fucking KEK. That's why I keep coming back to this cesspool of shit.
Btw, there is a city called "Pol" above them, LMAO

61971650
heard Iran is using boat drones now to blow up ships. based if true.

61971723
>>61969511
>we hate your government. It is evil. And you're evil for supporting it. Regardless of your religion.

61971801
>>61969346
I don't like Iran but I hate Israel even more. OIL to $120 by MO tomorrow, by $150 MC Friday. kikes stay coping and seething, Hitler was right screenshot this

61972224
>>61970114

item.title: Do any of you LARP as uwu aristocrats or princesses at tea parties — 13 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do any of you LARP as uwu aristocrats or princesses at tea parties

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:44 UTC

OP

I almost like the idea of setting up a kawaii victorian style tea party more than I like wearing lolita. What kind of reactions do other girls give you when you start LARPing? I get the feeling most of them are anime and video game nerds, so they've seen their fair share of autism. Post tea party ideas and recipes ITT I guess

Early replies (first 3)

61970918
>>61970765
Don't use tea bags. It looks so ordinary and isn't good for the flavor either.

But you also shouldn't use a teapot with an internal strainer. Have the tea leaves loose in the teapot and have each person use an over-cup tea strainer when they pour their tea. Some of these strainers have a vintage look (or are genuinely old), so it adds to the vibe.

61970935
>>61970765
I’m not a /pmg/ fag but I love having sterling silver items. they feel so nice and fancy. one of these days when I have the cash sitting around I’m buy a full set of sterling silverware.

61970976
The autism on this board is really something

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61971098
>>61970765

>>>/cgl/

Tell the gyarus I say hello from the cigarettes & galmilk store, assuming there is even a gyaru thread up anymore lol. Sad to say it but my shitposts were the only things keeping those threads moving at a normal speed, which is so depressing.

I usually take my gf to tea houses. We don't actively LARP but she likes when I dress like a 'tough guy' (basically the more professional version of sleazecore/ gangster.) This usually translates to a suit jacket and some really exotic shades, or some Robert Graham shit. This creates an interesting effect where the tea house ladies always chat us up relentlessly, because they have no idea what the fuck I'm doing in their establishment. I've worn more casual "gyaruo" clothes there too, but I usually keep that attire limited to our mall or beach runs.

61971204
>>61970918
>he's not making his tea in one of those
Oh my, a genuine pleb

61971296
>>61971204
Beautiful

61971304
>>61970976
Kek

61971308
>>61971098
Where the fuck are there tea houses

61971373
>>61970765
How do i profit from this anon ?

61971616
>>61970765
I recently went for afternoon tea with my mother at the café of a five-star hotel.

61972183
I larp as a victorian boywife.

61972269
Oui oui! I am Marquez du Mellon!

61972311
SEE I FUCKING KNEW THERE WERE MORE /biz/litas OUT HERE THAN JUST ME!!

ASAFAFSASASDDSADS

I knew there had to be more of us out here because I've actually met 2 other /biz/litas IRL at /cgl/ meetups at conventions before. So it's not an impossible mix.

Henlo fren ^.^

I was actually wearing some jfash earlier today while staring at charts. (I try to wear jfash and lolita in my day-to-day as much as possible.) I was just at AMKE this past weekend and extended my stay till Tuesday so I could trade the oil gigapump Sunday night going into Monday morning. I was still wearing my cosplay that I wore on the con floor that Sunday while trading that night! :D

Go post this over on >>>/cgl/ since this thread will probabily fall off the 'log here in a few hours. Plus /cgl/ is soooo sloooooow it'll actually last over there :3 would be awesome to chat about the economics of running a comm and setting up tea parties and The Boots Economic Theory is why nobody should be buying "lolita" items from Temu and how the current (((situation))) will make importing jfash and lolita even more unattainable for more people.

AAAAAFAFSDSSDDSD *hugs u*

item.title: I'M ALL IN ON XRP (TICKER: XRP) — 17 replies — 31.5/hr

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-03-12 03:28 UTC

OP

I'M ALL IN ON XRP (TICKER: XRP)

Early replies (first 3)

61961661
>not buying at under 0.25
ngmi

61964644
>>61961135
Well add more money then

61964646
>>61961135
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61965287
>>61961135
Since you have posted it has started going down, thanks faggot

61965305
>>61965287
Time to buy some more. I'm still grinding towards viscount.

61965356
>>61961135
I'M ALL IN ON UNITED STATES DOLLAR (TICKER: USD)

61965378
>>61961135
SO BULLISH XRP
(BULLISH ON XRP)

61969217
>>61965287
JUST BUY MORE XRP( TICKER:XRP)

61969257
>>61961135
DANGEROUSLY based

61970447
>>61961135
I don't want to find the object that's not like the other objects

61970613
>>61961135
>>61961135
just sold because of this post

61970638
Thanks for buying xrp fools.

61970643
>>61965305
i wonder what people with millions would be considered

61970652
>>61970643
emperors? overlords? archons? chuds?

61971034
>>61970652
God.

61972253
>>61961135
Based OP

61972260
>>61970638
In what world is this a bad thing? Ripple built their entire business around building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them. They don't dump on public exchange on retail and sell in blind auctions in a demand neutral way exclusively to clients and their sales account for <1% of total volume and impacts price literally zero. Ripple hold more than 2x their current total valuation in XRP reserves that are not even considered in their valuation and are BY FAR their largest asset. It is UNREAL how retarded faggots are trying to push this shit meanwhile their favorite projects like link literally dump directly on retail constantly and sell from operator rewards they promised were ring fenced and built zero utility for their token outside randomly inserting it in as a friction that most of the things people use them for doesn't even use in a deep cost way and is a completely unnecessary jewish usury which has no reason to exist and solves no problems or efficiencies and just makes everything less good to engrain a jewish rent seeking fee in the middle of the tech revolution that is aimed at killing rent seekers. Insane projection and endless cope and morally unconscionable literal cultists.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 44 replies — 24.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-03-12 02:11 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757

61933779
>>61932834

i dont sell my dotty

61936705

61962633

61962637

61962645

"
$TDOT is the first-ever @Polkadot

It’s official.
The first Polkadot U.S. ETF is live and trading on Nasdaq.
"

61962682
>>61962633

61962686

61962688

61962726

61966637

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61971966
>>61971964

61971967
>>61971964

Genuine question.

61972001
>>61971967
>What did he mean by this?


>BlackRock (BLK) Stock Plunges 5% as $26B Private Credit Fund Limits Investor Exits
https://blockonomi.com/blackrock-blk-stock-plunges-5-as-26b-private-credit-fund-limits-investor-exits/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61972006
>>61972001
What’s that mean

61972009
>>61971967
Make Netflix cast niggers in historically accurate period pieces.

61972012
>>61972006
They aren't letting people withdraw funds from the casino because somewhere their balance and leverages can't cover it.

61972016
>>61971967
DEI and managed decline. All the stuff /pol/ says is happening that isn't happening but it is a good thing if it does.

61972021
>>61971967
Make everybody brown, gay, and retarded, because this is the only jews can dominate

61972023
https://youtu.be/KwwN5kwjAtQ

61972040
>>61972012
What’s that mean for us?

61972045
>>61972040
No refunds

61972051
>>61972040
You'll be bailing them out when they steal your retirement to make the frogs gay and the girl bosses empowered.

61972054
>>61972040
Nothing. The fund in question invests in illiquid assets and has a contractual withdrawal rate limit of 5% per quarter. Some investor tried to withdraw 9%, got told no, and went to the press crying about it and trying to make a stink.

61972057
>>61972001
>25 Billion in garbage credit
>13 Trillion dollar in assets under management

Cry me a river goyim

61972072
>>61972054
Is that what all that "Blackrock restricts investors' withdrawals¡!!1!" was about? It's always some stupid shit like that.

61972075
>>61972045
>>61972051
>>61972054

61972077
>>61971966

61972090

They don't dump on public exchange on retail and sell in blind auctions in a demand neutral way exclusively to clients and their sales account for <1% of total volume and impacts price literally zero.
